reviews.review_text: Wide open spaces. Plenty of room and space to wander. Drop toilets. Talbingo 8 mins drive for dump spot, potable water and fuel.

A very nice place as a free camp ground. Perfect for off grid campers. We had no probs with our 19’6” caravan. The place was apparently booked out but we had heaps of space around us. We chose a shaded area under the trees others were 1.2km from us near the waters edge. We gave it 4 star as it is quite dusty and the water levels at the moment of blowering reservoir are currently less than 30% with plenty of mud at the waters edge.

Nice camping spot. We saw emus and lots of kangaroos. Plenty of shade.
Dry (composting) toilets available.

Unfortunately, a group of people had a kind of party, with noise and music until almost midnight.

Lovely spot. Camp either under/ near trees or closer to water, plenty of room.
Magnificant views of dam. However very muddy & slippery down near waters edge. No area to launch kayaks or fish. Quiet and peaceful all the same.Be very treacherous in wet times with muddy areas.

This national park camp site was great. It cost us $6 a night. The droptoliets were clean and tidy (they were cleaned while we were there). There was so much room to camp and no one was really on top of each other. You can camp under the trees or you can camp down by the water. You get water views from wherever you’re camping. In the afternoon you can see kangraoos jumping around. If you have a camper or caravan there is a water tap and a dump area 7mins down the road at Talbingo. We would come here again. It was very dusty there.

Nice spot beside the lake, but highly avoid during peak season. Hundreds of vans everywhere, vehicles speeding, countless dogs, non stop noise and it seems that not paying the booking fee is the norm. Can't imagine how much lost revenue National Parks is missing out on. Some of that missing money could be used to build more toilet and rubbish facilities throughout the park.
Not the best, would be good to see some renewal of the facilities. A bit tired.
Lots of space during the week. Great place for boating.

Perfect spot for an overnighter ... bit cool and windy when we arrived but died down as the evening went on ... was lovely around the firepit ... lots of birds, kangaroos and spotted an emu when leaving this morning just up the road heading back to tumut

Yachting Point is a beautiful campground located on the eastern side of Blowering Reservoir, accessed via the Snowy Mountains Highway.
Because of its west facing aspect it offers stunning sunsets over the range and water.
Campers will need to be self sufficient as there is no power or water supply.
The campground is only a 10 minute drive from the township of Talbingo located to the south or a 25 minute drive to the larger town of Tumut to the north, both towns are accessed via the Snowy Mountains Highway.
This is a very popular campground and does become very busy during school holidays and long weekends.
Bookings are required via the national parks website.
There are lots of open spaces close to the water to set up or further back from the water offers the picnic table, shaded areas with lots of trees and the amenities.
Incredible campsite right by the water, we stayed in our van which looked super cool, surrounded in kangaroos, right by the water!
Water was low, fish weren't biting but otherwise it was good.
Large campsite right on the dam, easy boat access. It has the usual drop toilet facilities. Overall a lovely spot in great location.
